A0) Slot policy to reduce misuse (recommended defaults)

A1.2 Slot coverage: only add what solves real customization needs
A2) Button (RTextButton) — bring it to the same slot quality as dropdown
A2.1 Add decoratable slots (context + default child)
A3) Checkbox (RCheckbox, RCheckboxListTile) — minimal but practical slots
A3.1 Checkbox indicator slots (for real customization)
A3.2 CheckboxListTile slots: evolve to SlotOverride for safety
B1) Dropdown: Safe renderer builder (highest ROI)
B1.1 SafeDropdownRenderer — MVP scope (to prevent scope creep)
B2) Button + Checkbox: safe renderer scaffolds (lower ROI than dropdown)
Post-implementation gap analysis (what still blocks a 9.5–10/10)
G1) Public primitives accidentally own interaction (violates the primitives contract)
G2) SafeDropdownRenderer is not “structurally safe” against double-select yet
“Finish to 9.5–10/10” — completion plan (architectural, no scope creep)
F1) Public primitive owns gesture handling (must be made visual-only)
F2) SafeDropdownRenderer exposes selection callback to builders (double-select risk)
P1) Primitives: enforce “visual-only by default” without freezing UI decisions
P2) SafeDropdownRenderer: API that is safe by design (selection ownership)
P3) Minimum conformance tests (high ROI, non-negotiable)

TL;DR (what the end result looks like)
Spec-first: Headless = a standard for the ecosystem (and we only do it this way)
How we lock in research (to avoid doing "like they do")
Dependency Table (what each package is allowed to import)
Dual API policy (D2a now, path to D2b later)
Renderer contracts: capability discovery + API stability policy (v1)
Interaction layers + Owners (locked in, how to do it right)
Tokens pipeline v1: W3C -> headless tokens (no runtime parsing)
Governance v1: how we make architectural decisions
Typical Cases (where to put code so as not to break boundaries)
Case 1: "Dialog wants Button" (don't do dialog -> button)
Case 2: "DropdownButton wants Dialog/Overlay" (don't do dropdown -> dialog)
Case 3: "Multiple components want the same hover/pressed/focus logic"
Case 4: "Need unified state priority rules" (pressed vs disabled vs focused)
Case 5: "Want a shared visual pattern: buttons in a dialog look like this"
Case 7: "Seems like a shared UI component is needed (e.g., Divider/Label/Spinner)"
Threshold for extraction to headless foundation (the proper 2-3 features rule)
What "foundation" is (and what it is not)
Recommendation: lockstep versioning for all public packages

Controller approach + ValueListenable as the minimum common denominator
Immutable State Object + events for complex components
Effects / Overlay / Listbox - mandatory v1 contracts
"Transition interception" (analogue of stateReducer) - for product exceptions
